subcommands.description: Create an authorization rule for a notification hub

-n
options.description: Authorization Rule Name

options.name: --namespace-name
options.description: The namespace name

options.name: --notification-hub-name
options.description: The notification hub name

name: --resource-group
-g
options.description: Name of resource group. You can configure the default group using az configure --defaults group=<name>

options.name: --rights
options.description: The rights associated with the rule. Support shorthand-syntax, json-file and yaml-file. Try "??" to show more

subcommands.description: Delete a notificationHub authorization rule

options.description: The notification hub name

name: --resource-group
-g
options.description: Name of resource group. You can configure the default group using az configure --defaults group=<name>

options.name: --message
options.description: The message body to send. If not None, payload will be ignored

options.name: --payload
options.description: The payload for the message in JSON format

options.name: --tag
options.description: You can send test notifications to a specific set of registrations using this option. Leave this field empty if you like to send push notifications to 10 random registrations on the selected platform

options.name: --title
options.description: The title of the notification

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Update credential for Apple(APNS)

name: --notification-hub-name
--name
-n
options.description: The notification hub name

options.name: --namespace-name
options.description: The namespace name

name: --resource-group
-g
options.description: Name of resource group. You can configure the default group using az configure --defaults group=<name>

options.name: --apns-certificate
options.description: The APNS certificate

options.name: --app-id
options.description: The issuer (iss) registered claim key. The value is a 10-character TeamId, obtained from your developer account. Specify if using Token Authentication Mode

options.name: --app-name
options.description: The name of the application or BundleId. Specify if using Token Authentication Mode

options.name: --certificate-key
options.description: The APNS certificate password if it exists

options.name: --endpoint
options.description: The APNS endpoint of this credential. If using Certificate Authentication Mode and Sandbox specify 'gateway.sandbox.push.apple.com'. If using Certificate Authentication Mode and Production specify 'gateway.push.apple.com'. If using Token Authentication Mode and Sandbox specify 'https://api.development.push.apple.com:443/3/device'. If using Token Authentication Mode and Production specify 'https://api.push.apple.com:443/3/device'

options.name: --key-id
options.description: A 10-character key identifier (kid) key, obtained from your developer account. Specify if using Token Authentication Mode

options.name: --token
options.description: Provider Authentication Token, obtained through your developer account. Specify if using Token Authentication Mode

options.description: Baidu Secret Key

subcommands.name: wait
subcommands.description: Place the CLI in a waiting state until a condition is met

options.name: --created
options.description: Wait until created with 'provisioningState' at 'Succeeded'

options.name: --custom
options.description: Wait until the condition satisfies a custom JMESPath query. E.g. provisioningState!='InProgress', instanceView.statuses[?code=='PowerState/running']

options.name: --deleted
options.description: Wait until deleted

options.name: --exists
options.description: Wait until the resource exists

options.name: --ids
options.description: One or more resource IDs (space-delimited). It should be a complete resource ID containing all information of 'Resource Id' arguments. You should provide either --ids or other 'Resource Id' arguments

options.name: --interval
options.description: Polling interval in seconds

-n
options.description: The namespace name

name: --resource-group
-g
options.description: Name of resource group. You can configure the default group using az configure --defaults group=<name>

options.name: --subscription
options.description: Name or ID of subscription. You can configure the default subscription using az account set -s NAME_OR_ID

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Maximum wait in seconds

options.name: --updated
options.description: Wait until updated with provisioningState at 'Succeeded'
